description: When you hear these keywords in a problem statement, your brain should instantly light up with 'Factory pattern!'

triggers.example: Create a vehicle based on type -- car, bike, truck
Generate a report depending on format -- PDF, Excel, CSV
The system has multiple types of payments
Dynamically create the right handler at runtime
Client shouldn't know the exact class being instantiated

triggers.keyword: easy to add new types in the future
triggers.example: Design it so adding a new notification channel is easy

notStrategy.keyword: swap behavior at runtime on same object
notStrategy.reason: That's algorithm swapping on an existing object, not creation of new objects
Builder configures HOW to build. Factory decides WHICH type to build.

notStrategy.keyword: families of related objects that must stay consistent
notStrategy.reason: Abstract Factory creates families. Simple/Method Factory creates one product type.

litmusTest: If the QUESTION is 'which class should I instantiate?' and the answer depends on runtime input, think Factory.
